cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out what the answer is here. The question is asking what Lao Yang hoped for with his article. The options are A, B, C, or D. First, let me recap the story. Lao Yang grew up in a small town and loved reading. He wanted to be a writer because he thought writers were respected and made a lot of money. However, when he was younger, he sent stories to editorial departments but never got any responses. Now he works in a factory and is busy, but he still reads when he has free time. He hasn't given up on his dream of being a writer. Then, his daughter Xiao Ping comes home worried because her teacher, Miss Gao, assigned an essay titled "My Father" and she doesn't know what to write. Lao Yang offers to help her and writes the essay himself, believing that it's good and that Miss Gao will like it. He's confident enough to ask his daughter later if the article was chosen to be sent to the editorial department. However, the teacher said the article was off-topic. But the key part here is why Lao Yang wrote the article. He did it to help his daughter with her school assignment, but he also saw it as an opportunity to possibly submit his work again to become a writer. The question is about what he hoped—what was his intention when he wrote the article. The options are: A) his article could surprise the teacher B) his article could be chosen C) the children could like his article D) everyone could soon know him Looking at the story, Lao Yang wrote the essay for his daughter, but he also wanted it to be good enough that Miss Gao would like it. The fact that he asked his daughter if it was chosen to be sent to the editorial department suggests that he wanted the article to be selected for publication. So his hope was that the article would be chosen (option B). He didn't necessarily want to surprise the teacher (A), as he was confident in his work.
